# The Two Johns Framework

Carmack and Romero: the partnership that created the first-person shooter.

**Case: The Ferrari. After Doom, the team bought matching red Ferraris. The peak of success.**

**Case: The Shareware. Giving away the first episode of Doom for free built a massive fan base.**

**Case: The Engine. Carmack's Doom engine was a masterpiece of optimization.**

**Case: The Split. Romero wanted story. Carmack wanted speed. The partnership broke.**

**Case: The Legacy. Doom created the FPS genre. It changed gaming forever.**
---

*Generated by [Heardly App](https://www.heard.ly) — turning books into knowledge you can Listen and Execute.*

**Case: The Columbine. Doom was blamed for the Columbine shooting. A controversy that still echoes.**
**Case: The Oculus. Carmack joined Oculus VR, pushing the next frontier.**
**Case: The Daikatana. Romero's post-Id game was a critical and commercial failure.**
**Case: The Reunion. Years later, the Two Johns reconciled.**
**Case: The Mods. Doom encouraged players to create their own levels. User-generated content was born.**
**Case: The NIN. Trent Reznor of Nine Inch Nails contributed music to Quake.**
**Case: The Conventions. Doom conventions became legendary. Gamers gathered to compete.**
**Case: The Engine License. Carmack licensed the Doom engine to other developers. It became a business.**

**Case: The Assembly Language. Carmack wrote the Doom renderer in assembly for maximum speed.**
